topics.title: Literals & Metacharacters
topics.description: Matching literal text and the special meaning of . ^ $ * + ? { } [ ] \ | ( ).

topics.title: Character Classes
topics.description: [abc], [a-z], [^abc] for matching sets of characters.

topics.title: Predefined Character Classes
topics.description: \d, \w, \s, \D, \W, \S and their Unicode behavior.

topics.title: Alternation with |
topics.description: Matching one of several alternatives with the pipe operator.

topics.title: Anchors (^ and $)
topics.description: ^ for start-of-string, $ for end-of-string, and \b word boundary.

topics.title: Greedy Quantifiers
topics.description: *, +, ?, {n}, {n,m} — greedy matching and backtracking.

topics.title: Lazy (Non-Greedy) Quantifiers
topics.description: *?, +?, ?? for minimal matching.

topics.title: Possessive Quantifiers & Atomic Groups
topics.description: Preventing backtracking for performance-critical patterns.

topics.title: Capturing Groups
topics.description: (pattern) for capturing submatches and back-references.

topics.title: Non-Capturing Groups
topics.description: (?:pattern) for grouping without capturing.

topics.title: Named Groups
topics.description: (?P<name>pattern) and (?P=name) back-references in Python.

topics.title: re.match() vs re.search()
topics.description: match() anchors to string start; search() scans entire string.

topics.title: re.findall() & re.finditer()
topics.description: Finding all matches as a list or iterator.

topics.title: re.sub() & re.subn()
topics.description: Substituting patterns with replacement strings or functions.

topics.title: re.compile() & Flags
topics.description: Compiling patterns for reuse and re.IGNORECASE, MULTILINE, DOTALL flags.

topics.title: Email & URL Validation
topics.description: Regex patterns for validating email addresses and URLs.

topics.title: Data Extraction Patterns
topics.description: Extracting dates, IPs, phone numbers, and structured data from text.

topics.title: Log Parsing with Regex
topics.description: Parsing structured log lines, timestamps, and error messages.

topics.title: Regex in Pandas & SQL
topics.description: Using regex with str.extract(), str.contains() in Pandas and REGEXP in SQL.
